title: Kara L. Smyth, KC
content:
Partner | Food, Beverage & Agribusiness Sector Lead
People.ContactEmail ksmyth@mccarthy.ca
- University of Saskatchewan
A relentless advocate for her clients, Kara combines strategic insight and courtroom credibility to resolve the most complex and high‑stakes disputes.
Kara Smyth is a partner in our Litigation & Dispute Resolution Group in Calgary, co-lead of the Firm’s Food and Agribusiness Group, and a member of the Firm’s Board of Partners. Renowned for her innovative approach to handling complex business disputes, her practice focuses primarily on corporate-commercial, class action, and public law litigation. Kara represents clients in a number of fora and across a diverse range of industries, including energy, renewables, agribusiness, commercial real estate, retail, and technology. Her experience includes disputes involving commercial contracts, product liability, environmental claims, data compensation, judicial reviews, and complex commercial transactions. Appointed King’s Counsel (KC) in 2026, Kara is recognized for excellence in the practice of law and a demonstrated commitment to community service.
Kara frequently advises on court proceedings that attract media attention and works closely with in-house teams and communications advisors to ensure litigation strategy aligns with broader business and reputational objectives. Clients value her creativity, responsiveness, and deep industry knowledge. Many of her engagements are resolved creatively outside the litigation process through early case development and identifying business risks relevant to the dispute and the client’s broader commercial objectives. Kara has appeared before all levels of court in Alberta and the Federal Court in complex interlocutory motions, judicial reviews, trials, and appeals.
Her work has been widely recognized — she was named 2025 Alberta Litigator of the Year and was part of the team awarded Impact Case of the Year by Benchmark Litigation Canada, reflecting her role in leading precedent-setting, high-stakes disputes.

#### S elect reported decisions:
- Egale Canada v Alberta , 2025 ABKB 394
- Enmax Corporation v Independent System Operator (Alberta Electric Systems Operator) , 2023 ABKB 191; 2024 ABCA 83
- Dione Setoguchi v Uber B.V. , 2021 ABQB 18; 2023 ABCA 45; 2023 CanLII 62020 (SCC)
- Smith v Lafarge Canada Inc. , 2022 ABQB 289
- Re Bison Acquisition Corp. , 2021 ABASC 188
- Underhill v Shell Canada Limited , 2020 ABQB 342
- Allergan Inc. v Alberta (Justice and Solicitor General), 2019 ABQB 610; 2021 ABCA 32
- Semex Alliance v Hi-Tech Dairy Equipment Inc. , 2019 ABQB 70
- Aquatech v Alberta (Minister of Environment and Parks) , 2019 ABQB 62
- Potvin v Rooke , 2019 FCA 285
- Clearview Plumbing & Heating Ltd. and Giraffe Corp v Clockwork IP, LLC et al , 2018 FC 169, 156 C.P.R. (4th) 321
- Potvin v Prowse et al , (8 Jul 2018), Calgary T-83-18 (FC)
- David Suzuki v Syngenta , 2017 FC 682, 13 C.E.L.R. (4th) 215
- City of Edmonton v Uber , 2015 ABQB 214

#### Additional representative experience:
- Sandra Furlonger v Lafarge Canada Inc., ABKB Court File No. 2301-16311 – counsel to Lafarge Canada Inc. in the defence of certification of a putative class action relating to operations at Lafarge’s cement plant in Exshaw, Alberta, involving allegations of nuisance, strict liability, and negligence.
- Gerald Smith v Lafarge Canada Inc. , ABKB Court File No. 2101-02829 - counsel to Lafarge Canada Inc. in the defence of certification of a putative class action alleging underpayment of employee benefits.
- Cleaver-Brooks v CNRL et al , ABKB Court File No. 1601-04311 – counsel to Cleaver‑Brooks in complex multi‑party litigation involving claims for unpaid invoices and the defence of counterclaims alleging design deficiencies in industrial boilers used in CNRL’s SAGD operations.
- The City of Medicine Hat and LGX Oil + Gas Inc. v Attorney General of Canada, The Minister of the Environment and the Governor General in Council , FC No. T-12-14; ABQB No. 1401-10147 – counsel for the City of Medicine Hat in a judicial review and constitutional challenge of the first-ever emergency protection order issued under the Species at Risk Act , and in related proceedings seeking compensation based on de facto expropriation and injurious affection principles.
- Allergan Inc. v Alberta (Minister of Justice and Solicitor General) et al , ABQB No. 1701-05590 – counsel for Allergan on judicial review of a decision of the Alberta Minister of Health regarding the interchangeability of certain glaucoma drugs.
- Debby Allen v Direct Energy Marketing Ltd , ABQB No. 0901-12817 – defence and resolution of a $60 million class action relating to late payment penalty practices.
- Alzu et al v Smucker Foods of Canada Corp. et al, ABQB No. 1703-07203 – counsel for Smucker in putative class action involving alleged E. coli contamination of flour.
- Numerous complex commercial arbitrations involving joint venture, licence, and supply agreements, as well as data compensation claims under the Pest Control Products Act .
Outside of the courtroom, Kara contributes to numerous industry publications and blogs, and is a contributing author of the sixth edition of Defending Class Actions in Canada (LexisNexis).
Deeply committed to mentorship and community impact, Kara mentors lawyers within the firm and has mentored youth through the Martin Family Initiative, a program focused on improving education outcomes for Indigenous people in Canada. She also serves as pro bono counsel to Egale, Skipping Stone, and gender-diverse youth and their parents, acting as lead counsel in pursuing a constitutional challenge to Alberta’s ban on certain gender-affirming medical treatments and amendments to Alberta’s Education Amendment Act .
Kara is active in the Alberta soccer community, and served as Secretary and a Board member of Calgary Villains FC for 15 years.
Kara graduated from the University of Saskatchewan, clerked at the Court of Appeal of Alberta and the Court of King’s Bench, and was called to the Alberta Bar in 2001.
